methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Natilus is a Series A-stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The target company has raised $16M to date. The comparable universe in Drones & Autonomous comprises 8 private companies, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker; no public comps exist in this narrow segment.

The valuation analysis applies Stage-Progression methodology, which is applicable given the Series A context. Using total raised ($16M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $32M. Peer-Median triangulation was not applied; although 4 same-sector peers exist, the filtered peer set (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier) contained fewer than 3 comparable datapoints, making sector-wide median unreliable and potentially misleading.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $27M (low) to $37M (high), with a median point of $32M. This narrow range reflects reliance on one methodology rather than cross-validation across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of close-stage peer data for calibration, limited public market benchmarks in the autonomous systems space, and the stage-progression multiplier's applicability to this specific company's growth profile and risk factors. Validation through revenue trajectory, customer traction, or burn metrics would strengthen conviction in this range.
